bits.premise: Racism can mean loyalty to the human race.
Legal weed erases prohibition-era weed rituals.

lines.text: After a bit of self-reflection, turns out I too am racist.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Because if you're not the human race, I don't like you.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: I don't like AI. I don't like robots. I don't like humanoids.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: I don't like cyborgs, waymos, or Roombas.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: Basically, if you ain't got blood vessels, we ain't friends.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: What do I look like hanging out with a bunch of wired beans?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: If I want to be around a bunch of wired beans,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I'd have kept cooking meth.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Speaking of drugs, they've been talking about
lines.label: setup

lines.text: legalizing marijuana in America,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and I've got one problem with that.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: This new generation will never understand the prohibition.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: They will never remember peeling apart Mexican brick weed.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: They won't know the frustration of sifting seeds
lines.label: setup

lines.text: for five minutes to roll a joint.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: They won't even know what it tastes like
lines.label: setup

lines.text: to smoke the resin out of your bong,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: 'cause that asshole won't answer his phone.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Other than that, I'm okay with it.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: That's my time.
lines.label: fluff
